operating_state: Total net sales declined 22% to $44.8 billion driven by lower shipment volumes across all equipment segments amid deteriorating market conditions. Production and precision agriculture volumes declined broadly with double-digit decreases globally, while small agriculture and turf experienced similar volume contractions. Construction and forestry saw more moderate declines domestically with smaller international reductions. Financial services revenue increased as higher interest rates improved financing spreads despite lower equipment volumes.
cost_structure: Cost of sales ratio increased to 68.8% from 67.9% driven by higher overhead costs from reduced volumes creating production inefficiencies, partially offset by sales price realization and lower material costs. Operating margins compressed across all segments with production and precision agriculture declining to 21.7%. Employee separation programs generated pretax expenses targeting annual savings while research and development expenses increased supporting continued technology advancement initiatives.
material_events: Implemented global employee separation programs affecting salaried workforce with separation costs recorded in 2024. Entered joint venture agreement with Banco Bradesco for 50% ownership of Brazilian subsidiary expected to close in first half of 2025. Repurchased $4.0 billion of common stock with $8.9 billion remaining under authorization. Agreed to pay $10.0 million to SEC resolving FCPA violations from improper payments by Wirtgen Thailand subsidiary.
macro_exposures: Elevated interest rates continued negatively impacting customer borrowing costs and product demand while affecting financing spreads for financial services operations. Agricultural market cycle deterioration resulted in lower sales volumes, higher sales incentives, and increased receivable write-offs. Currency controls in Argentina restrict access to U.S. dollars and earnings repatriation creating long-term exposure. Market uncertainty intensified following 2024 U.S. presidential elections creating potential shifts in trade and economic policies alongside ongoing geopolitical tensions.
forward_outlook: Agriculture and turf equipment sales projected to decline in 2025 due to global market contraction with demand expected to moderate further amid weak farm fundamentals and high interest rates. Construction equipment sales anticipated to decline as healthy end markets are offset by continued purchase uncertainty. Management continues pursuing Smart Industrial Operating Model targeting enhanced customer economic value through digitalization, autonomy, automation, and alternative power technologies including expanding Solutions as a Service business model.
uncertainty_profile: Credit loss provisions increased to $310 million from negative $16 million reflecting deteriorating agricultural economic conditions and higher expected defaults. Federal Trade Commission investigation ongoing regarding repair service practices and monopolization allegations in consolidated multidistrict class action lawsuit. Collective bargaining agreements covering U.S. employees expire between 2025 and 2027. Potential radio frequency band reallocations could disrupt GPS-based precision agriculture systems while environmental compliance costs may increase from stricter emissions regulations.
